description: Remove external network dependency from E2E tests by replacing https://example.com navigations with locally-served test pages, making tests reliable in network-restricted CI environments.

userStories.title: Add a non-matching page endpoint to the test server
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the test server to serve a minimal HTML page at /non-matching that can be used as a substitute for example.com in tests that verify adapter non-injection on non-matching URLs.
acceptanceCriteria: The test server serves a minimal HTML page at GET /non-matching with a distinct <title> and body text
The page does NOT match the e2e-test plugin's URL pattern (the plugin matches the test server's root, so /non-matching should still match since it's the same origin — see notes)
userStories.notes:
File: e2e/test-server.ts. The e2e-test plugin's URL match pattern is defined in plugins/e2e-test/src/index.ts — read it to understand what URLs it matches. The plugin likely matches `*://localhost:PORT/*` or similar, which means ALL paths on the test server would match. The tests that use example.com (tool-dispatch.e2e.ts:613, browser-tools.e2e.ts:283/312/355, dispatch-resilience.e2e.ts:238, tab-state-sync.e2e.ts:70) need a URL that does NOT match the e2e-test plugin's URL pattern. Since the test server is on localhost and the plugin matches localhost, simply using a different path won't work. Possible approaches: 1. Use a data: URL like `data:text/html,

Non-matching

` — this is a different scheme that no plugin URL pattern would match 2. Use about:blank — simple and reliable, doesn't require network 3. Use the test server on a different hostname (e.g., 127.0.0.1 vs localhost) if the plugin only matches 'localhost' 4. Use a chrome:// URL (some are loadable) The RIGHT approach depends on what the plugin's URL pattern is. Read plugins/e2e-test/src/index.ts to determine the match pattern, then decide the best approach. The goal is: a page that loads reliably without network AND does not match the e2e-test plugin pattern. IMPORTANT: If the simplest approach is to use about:blank or a data: URL, you do NOT need to add a test server endpoint at all — just update the test files directly. Adjust the story scope accordingly.

userStories.title: Replace example.com with local alternative in tool-dispatch and tab-state-sync tests
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the non-matching tab tests in tool-dispatch.e2e.ts and tab-state-sync.e2e.ts to use a local page instead of https://example.com so they work without external network access.
acceptanceCriteria: tool-dispatch.e2e.ts line ~613 no longer navigates to https://example.com
tab-state-sync.e2e.ts line ~70 no longer navigates to https://example.com
The replacement URL loads reliably without external network access
The replacement URL does NOT match the e2e-test plugin URL pattern
The non-injection assertion still correctly verifies that the adapter is not injected
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/tool-dispatch.e2e.ts line ~613: `page.goto('https://example.com', ...)` in 'adapter is NOT injected into non-matching tabs'
- e2e/tab-state-sync.e2e.ts line ~70: `page.goto('https://example.com', ...)` in 'navigate away closes tab state'

Replace with whatever local approach US-001 determined (about:blank, data: URL, etc.). Ensure the negative assertion still works — the adapter should not be injected into the replacement page.

userStories.title: Replace example.com with local alternative in browser-tools.e2e.ts
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want browser_open_tab tests to use a local page instead of https://example.com/example.org so they work without external network access.
acceptanceCriteria: All references to https://example.com and https://example.org in browser-tools.e2e.ts are replaced with local alternatives
Tests that open/close/navigate tabs still verify the same behavior
Tests that check tab URLs verify the correct new URLs
userStories.notes:
File: e2e/browser-tools.e2e.ts. References at lines ~283, 312, 355, 363 (example.org), 948, 972, 976, 1023.

These tests use example.com for browser_open_tab, browser_close_tab, browser_navigate_tab, browser_get_tab_info, and cookie tests. They need a URL that:
1. Loads reliably without network
2. Returns a valid HTML page (some tests check tab title, page content)
3. For cookie tests: supports setting/reading cookies (about:blank may not work for cookies)

For browser tool tests, the test server's /interactive page or a new simple page endpoint would work well. The test server is already running and serves pages on localhost with ephemeral ports. Use testServer.url + a path for most tests. For cookie tests specifically, ensure the URL supports cookies.

The two example.org references (line 363, 976) are used to test navigating to a DIFFERENT domain — use the test server on 127.0.0.1 vs localhost (different origins) or another approach that doesn't need external network.

userStories.title: Replace example.com with local alternative in dispatch-resilience.e2e.ts
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want dispatch-resilience tests to use local pages instead of https://example.com so they work without external network access.
acceptanceCriteria: dispatch-resilience.e2e.ts line ~238 no longer navigates to https://example.com
dispatch-resilience.e2e.ts line ~591 no longer references https://example.com
The replacement URLs load reliably without external network
Tests still verify the same dispatch resilience behaviors
userStories.notes:
File: e2e/dispatch-resilience.e2e.ts.
- Line ~238: `page.goto('https://example.com', ...)` in 'tool dispatch after navigating away' — navigates to a non-matching URL to test that tools fail when the tab is on a non-matching page.
- Line ~591: `url: 'https://example.com'` in a browser_open_tab call — used in the URL validation rejection test.

For line 238: replace with the same local non-matching URL from US-001/002.
For line 591: this is used in a test about URL validation/rejection — check whether the test is validating that dangerous URLs (javascript:, data:, file:) are rejected. If it's just using example.com as a benign URL to open, replace with the test server URL. If it's testing something specific about external URLs, may need a different approach.
